title: Education for Orphans !
overview_text: A new school year has arrived, bringing with it colors of joy, knocking on doors and whispering in the ears of children: "Get ready, I have come!" Mothers have begun lovingly preparing clothes and school supplies, looking forward to a year filled with knowledge, effort, and happiness. But on the other side, there stand orphaned children, hearts gripped by pain, unable to afford even the most basic school essentials. From here, we launch our campaign to provide back-to-school supplies and bring smiles to the hearts of our orphaned children before their faces. Share value: only $25–$30 The campaign will support our orphans in Turkey and Syria, and the amount will be delivered in cash directly to the child, allowing them to purchase their school needs with freedom and dignity.

title: Douma National Hospital — Our Countryside Deserves It Campaign
overview_text:
Douma National Hospital — Our Countryside Deserves It Campaign
This campaign aims to provide financial and in-kind support to complete the preparation of Douma National Hospital and put it back into service after it was destroyed during the years of the revolution by the former regime. The hospital was the central surgical facility for the city of Douma and most of the towns in Rural Damascus Governorate. Reactivating Douma National Hospital and returning it to service will provide medical services to approximately 500,000 residents in the city and surrounding areas. Short-Term Plan: This phase includes urgent measures required to get the hospital operational. The plan can be completed within one month if sufficient support is available. Key requirements for this phase: - General site preparation: outer fence, lighting, paving internal roads, gates, landscaping, 2 elevators - 2 power generators - Archive setup and computers - Morgue refrigerator - Installation of 2 heating boilers - Total cost for this phase: $300,000–$400,000 - Estimated 6-month consumables cost: $100,000 Medium-Term Plan: This phase includes measures to launch the hospital in a way that meets the actual needs of the area. It can be implemented within 6 months to 1 year if support is available. Measures for this phase include: - Construction of a surgical block for general, orthopedic, and specialized surgeries - Main building allocation for pediatrics, obstetrics, and internal medicine - Staff housing construction - Restaurant/cafeteria setup - Total cost: $500,000–$600,000 Long-Term Plan: This phase includes advanced measures to expand services, including: - Training center with nursing and anesthesia institutes under the Ministry of Health - MRI machine - Construction of an internal medicine block - Construction of a clinics building - This phase can be completed within 1–2 years if support is available. - Estimated cost: approximately $1,000,000
details_text: This campaign aims to provide financial and in-kind support to complete the preparation of Douma National Hospital and put it back into service after it was destroyed during the years of the revolution by the former regime. The hospital was the central surgical facility for the city of Douma and most of the towns in Rural Damascus Governorate. Reactivating Douma National Hospital and returning it to service will provide medical services to approximately 500,000 residents in the city and surrounding areas. Short-Term Plan: This phase includes urgent measures required to get the hospital operational. The plan can be completed within one month if sufficient support is available. Key requirements for this phase: - General site preparation: outer fence, lighting, paving internal roads, gates, landscaping, 2 elevators - 2 power generators - Archive setup and computers - Morgue refrigerator - Installation of 2 heating boilers - Total cost for this phase: $300,000–$400,000 - Estimated 6-month consumables cost: $100,000 Medium-Term Plan: This phase includes measures to launch the hospital in a way that meets the actual needs of the area. It can be implemented within 6 months to 1 year if support is available. Measures for this phase include: - Construction of a surgical block for general, orthopedic, and specialized surgeries - Main building allocation for pediatrics, obstetrics, and internal medicine - Staff housing construction - Restaurant/cafeteria setup - Total cost: $500,000–$600,000 Long-Term Plan: This phase includes advanced measures to expand services, including: - Training center with nursing and anesthesia institutes under the Ministry of Health - MRI machine - Construction of an internal medicine block - Construction of a clinics building - This phase can be completed within 1–2 years if support is available. - Estimated cost: approximately $1,000,000

She opened her heart before her doors, cradling the displaced, the wounded, and the hopeless in her embrace. Everyone who entered her arms came out more alive than when they arrived.

is a campaign to repay a heavy debt upon our shoulders. For we are not mere passersby to her pain; we are witnesses to it. Join us, donate, and take part… for Idlib, who once saved our dreams, deserves from us nothing less than loyalty equal to her patience. 💙

title: Feeding the Needy!
overview_text: Aunt Aisha, a grandmother worn down by the war, lives with her missing son’s children in a modest home surrounded by need on all sides. These little children are innocent, their only “fault” being that they were born into a reality that lacks even the most basic necessities. Aunt Aisha says, her voice breaking with sorrow: "I swear, my son, most days of the week we survive on dry bread that the children collect from neighbors, and when we finally have enough for a loaf of bread, it feels like a celebration." These children’s small bodies struggle with malnutrition, just like thousands of other families facing the same hardships. Mothers do the impossible to feed their children, children go to sleep hungry, and simple dreams fade in the harsh reality of hunger. This story is not just a tale, it reflects the reality that many of our people live today. For them, we launched the “Feed the Needy” campaign to reach these forgotten families and give their children the right to food. Join us in changing their lives, as every food basket delivered is a ray of hope for a child who deserves to grow up with dignity.

title: Ongoing Charity – Providing Water
overview_text:
Ongoing Charity – Providing Water
In the village of New Zizoun in rural Hama, people are still struggling to access one of the most basic necessities of life: water. For years, the village’s water station has been out of service due to theft and destruction. Residents now rely on water delivered by trucks from neighboring villages at very high prices, often beyond their means, and the water is frequently of poor quality and unsafe to drink. This crisis puts the lives of more than 4,200 people at risk every day and places a heavy burden on families trying to rebuild their lives after years of displacement and hardship. Returning to their homes without water means continuing suffering and delaying the dream of stability. From this reality, the Molham volunteer team launches the Revive Zizoun Well campaign, aiming to rehabilitate the well and operate it using a solar-powered system with a central tank capable of meeting the daily and sustainable needs of the community. Providing water is not just a service — it is a basic right and a foundation for a dignified life. By reviving the Zizoun Well, we give hope to the residents and take a real step toward bringing life back to their village. Dozens of villages in Syria still need water, and your donation could be the one thing that saves them!

title: Micro Projects !
overview_text: After years of injustice and displacement, after losing everything except their willpower, they now stand at the doorstep of hope—waiting for a helping hand to reach out to them. Thousands of families have lost their homes, breadwinners, and everything they owned, yet they have not lost their skills or experience. All they need is one opportunity to start again. Majed was released from Saydnaya Prison after years of suffering. When he returned to his family, he found his home destroyed and his life frozen at zero. Today, Majed dreams of opening a small clothing shop to support his family with dignity, but all he needs is a modest amount of capital for a new beginning. That’s why we launched the Micro Projects Campaign — to give Majed and countless others the chance to become productive members of their communities once again, earning their livelihoods with their own hands. Your support is the relief they’ve been waiting for. Together, we can turn their suffering into productivity, and their dreams into a reality they live every day.

title: Rastan day
overview_text: The first city to break the wall of fear, where its officers bravely defected from the tyrannical regime. It paid a heavy price under relentless bombardment and siege, until every neighborhood stood as a witness to unyielding resilience. Years of pain never extinguished the flame of dignity in its people's hearts — instead, they made Al-Rastan a symbol of defiance and steadfastness. For Al-Rastan, for its free people who endured until liberation, we launch this campaign today — to stand with them, to ease their wounds, and to repay, even in part, the debt we owe to this city that never bowed.

title: School desk
overview_text:
In some schools across Syria today, children do not sit on chairs… but on the cold ground.
The ground bites their small bodies with every wave of cold, as they try to learn… to write… to dream!
A boy places his notebook on his knees, and a girl leans her back against a crumbling wall,
yet their eyes — despite everything — still shine with hope.
This is the reality of our students in the affected areas…
Damp floors, and little backs that ache from sitting too long without a chair.
As winter begins, we want to tell them:
Your donation today, no matter how small, can bring
warmth to a child, a chair for learning, and hope for the future.

title: Dawn of Al - Qusair
overview_text:
Dawn of Al - Qusair
In 2013, the city was subjected to a large-scale military assault that led to massive destruction, forced displacement, and the emptying of neighborhoods and farmland. Despite the siege and bombardment, the people of Al-Qusair resisted with extraordinary courage, losing thousands of loved ones and enduring years of hardship. Homes were destroyed, orchards were wiped out, and the city was turned into a closed military zone where families were prevented from returning for more than ten years. On December 8, 2024, the people of Al-Qusair finally returned to their hometown after it was reopened. What they found was heartbreaking: a city with ruined infrastructure, no electricity, no clean water, no schools, and land that had not been cultivated for years. From this devastation, hope was born again through “Fajr al-Qusair” — a volunteer, community-led crowdfunding initiative created by the city’s people to rebuild what was destroyed: ✅ restoring schools and education ✅ reviving healthcare services ✅ repairing basic infrastructure ✅ supporting displaced families returning home Al-Qusair today stands as a living symbol of resilience — proof that even after destruction and displacement, a city can rise again through the strength of its people. Your support helps families rebuild their homes, their lives, and their future. Let the dawn rise again over Al-Qusair.

title: Soul Campaign
overview_text: Khadija’s life hangs on medication Khadija came into this world carrying an illness that weighed heavily on her parents’ hearts before she could even understand the meaning of childhood. She suffers from intellectual and motor atrophy, a condition that makes her life dependent on medication, treatment, and constant medical care—just to remain medically stable. Her body is fragile, her bones weak. She needs special milk to strengthen her body and give her a chance to grow. Yet this milk remains out of reach, because its cost exceeds the capacity of a family already exhausted by poverty. Her father, Hamdou, works as a daily wage laborer. Every morning he leaves in search of an income to feed his children, and often returns burdened by helplessness. His earnings are barely enough to secure bread—how could he possibly afford the cost of medication and urgent medical needs that cannot wait? Your contribution today could mean life. It could be the difference between ongoing suffering and the birth of new hope. Be the support Khadija is waiting for— and for her family, who have nothing left but patience.

title: In Loyalty to Kafranbel — Rebuilding Hope, Restoring Life
overview_text:
In Loyalty to Kafranbel — Rebuilding Hope, Restoring Life
In honor of a city that stood firm in word and in spirit — a city that defied silence with messages that reached the world and sacrificed its sons for freedom and dignity — it is now our turn to stand by her, to bring life back to the land that was stripped of it. The “Loyalty to Kafranbel” Campaign aims to rebuild what was destroyed from the ground up: Rehabilitating roads and infrastructure so that footsteps may return to their paths. Restoring mosques and community centers so that hearts and souls may reunite. Renovating more than 16 schools to bring back education and hope to children. Rebuilding public institutions and essential services to revive daily life. Reviving agriculture, so that the land of figs and olives may flourish once again. Loyalty today means a promise — a promise to a city that gave everything. Let’s stand together to rebuild Kafranbel and return life to its people.

His suffering didn’t end there—after returning with his family from years of displacement, he found his home looted, adding pain to pain.

Your monthly sponsorship of the family for just $150 can restore hope to their lives, and give the girls a piece of the childhood stolen by disease.
Contribute now… every donation brings hope back and eases the pain of a family in need.

is enough to secure the children’s essential needs and ease a significant part of the hardship on their mother and grandfather.

Despite all the family’s efforts, even the most basic necessities remain a daily struggle beyond their ability, leaving the children without milk or diapers for days at a time.

Just $150 is enough to secure the children’s essential needs and ease a significant part of the hardship on their mother and grandfather.

title: For Aleppo !
overview_text:
Aleppo… the capital of the north and the beating heart of Syria.
A city that carried the history of civilization on its shoulders, standing tall in the face of injustice and paying the price of its dignity with destruction that touched every inch of its land.
Its ancient markets, historic neighborhoods, schools, hospitals, and homes… all bear the marks of devastation. Yet Aleppo, which endured every challenge, will not surrender today. Aleppo as its people knew it — and as the world will know it again…
For this reason, we launch today the campaign
, aimed at supporting service projects and improving the quality of life in this province. The campaign seeks to enhance community participation in rebuilding what the war has destroyed, restoring infrastructure, and developing the essential services needed by the people returning to their city.
Today we answer the call and fulfill our promise… today we return to Aleppo its glory and its history.

title: Life 2
overview_text: Hassan is a young child who has not yet reached his third birthday. He was born with a painful immune disease that causes blisters and severe skin erosions across his body and limbs. Since coming into this world, our little child has been living with immense and constant pain. His suffering grows worse day after day due to his family’s inability to provide the necessary medical care, as well as their struggle to afford his essential medications. Today, Hassan is in severe pain, crying throughout the day, while his mother stands helpless before him, crying just like him, unable to ease his suffering. Little Hassan is in urgent need of medical examinations and medications to relieve his pain. An amount of $500 would be enough to cover these critical expenses. Please stand with us to help ease his pain and the suffering of dozens of children like him.

title: Mobile dental clinic
overview_text: In the remote countryside of Aleppo, thousands of children suffer silently from tooth pain. They cannot sleep because of their pain… and their smiles have turned into sighs of suffering. Their schools have never had a dental clinic visit them. That is why today, in partnership with the Syrian-Swedish Dental Association, we are launching the “Mobile Dental Clinic” project — a clinic that reaches them where doctors cannot. It will travel to rural schools, provide free dental check-ups, and spread health awareness among children. Your donation today is not just treatment… it is restoring a child’s smile. Because every child deserves a healthy smile and a future free from pain.

title: Winter Relief for Gaza !
overview_text:
Winter Relief for Gaza !
As Winter Approaches, Gaza Faces New Fears
As winter approaches, fears of tomorrow grow among our people in Gaza. The war has left them without a safe roof to protect them, and constant displacement has deprived them of sufficient clothing to shield their bodies from the cold.
Today, the children and adults of Gaza dread the arrival of winter. After all they have endured over the past two years, they can no longer bear another life-threatening hardship brought by a harsh winter.
, we strive to provide heating supplies, winter clothing, and tents to help our people in the Strip withstand the cold and its challenges.

title: Himam Campaign
overview_text: Thank you for your compassionate hearts that have restored hope to a child wearied by pain. Amina, who suffers from cerebral hypoxia, grew up in a modest home with her disabled grandfather and her aunts who are exhausted by the harshness of poverty, after she lost her mother and was abandoned by her father. She was unable to move, dreaming of taking just one step toward a kinder life. By the grace of Allah, and thanks to your generosity through the Special Needs Campaign, Amina was able to receive physiotherapy sessions for three months, beginning her first steps toward recovery. May Allah reward you with goodness. We pray that your generosity becomes a light along your paths and a mercy that fills your lives in this world and the hereafter. There are still many who wait for help, so be the support they need.

details_text:
Today, we launch the “Until the Last Tent – Partners in Return” campaign, a sustainable fund that provides interest-free loans to displaced families to help them rehabilitate their homes and return to them. This is implemented through an integrated program that relies on employing local community members in the reconstruction process, thereby reviving economic activity in villages that have been partially or completely destroyed.

The campaign is based on providing a home-rehabilitation loan to be repaid over five years. Employees and middle-income families are able to repay these loans from their salaries, while for the most vulnerable families, a portion of the loan can be covered through zakat and charitable donations. This approach ensures the fund’s financial independence and sustainability, enabling it to support a growing number of families each month.

This campaign aims to end the suffering of displacement camps and help families return to their homes, step by step, until not a single tent remains.

We invite you to become Partners in Return by donating and contributing to the establishment of this fund, and by making your contribution a lasting charity (ongoing charity) on your behalf or on behalf of your loved ones, so its impact continues for a lifetime.

details_text:
Aleppo is bleeding… and the displaced are without shelter.

Since yesterday, the city of Aleppo has been witnessing intense fighting. As a result, thousands of families have been forced to flee suddenly—leaving behind everything they own. They escaped without belongings, without shelter, and without any protection from the harsh cold and an even greater fear.

The situation cannot wait.
Every passing hour means more suffering for a family, a child, and a woman left without a roof over her head.

Your donation could be the warmth that protects them, the roof that shelters them, and the hope that shines through this darkness.

We are urgently working to provide:

  • 🍞 Essential food and basic supplies for families and children
  • 🧣 Winter blankets and clothing
  • 🔥 Emergency heating supplies
  • 🏕 Safe and temporary shelter

Thousands of women, children, and elderly people are now homeless—facing freezing winter conditions in the open. They stand on the edge of a severe humanitarian crisis that requires an immediate response with no room for delay.

Donate now.

title: Roof and Safety in Ramadan
overview_text:
Roof and Safety in Ramadan
In the displacement camps in Syria, thousands of families live in worn-out tents and temporary shelters that offer no protection from the summer heat or the winter cold. The “Roof and Safety” campaign is a humanitarian initiative aimed at rehabilitating and repairing damaged homes for families who have decided to leave these camps and return to their original hometowns. This will be achieved through: Providing essential building materials Repairing basic infrastructure Securing roofs that protect against rain and wind Creating safer and more dignified living spaces Dear brother, dear sister, while we enjoy the safety of our homes and the warmth of our families during Ramadan, hundreds of thousands in Syria’s camps are reminded that they have lost the most basic necessities of a dignified life. Your donation today is not just building material — it is: A roof protecting a child from the cold Dignity for a family that has lost everything A message of solidarity that eases the suffering of our brothers and sisters Ramadan is the month of compassion and empathy. Fasting makes us realize the value of food and drink, while also reminding us of those who do not have a roof over their heads. Repairing homes during this blessed month carries a special Ramadan meaning. Your donation today can change the life of an entire family. Join us in the “Roof and Safety” campaign and be part of restoring hope and security to those who have lost their homes. Every donation, big or small, is a brick in building a new life.

title: Paying Off the Debts of the Indebted!
overview_text:
Paying Off the Debts of the Indebted!
Debt weighs heavily on thousands of families who emerged from war exhausted and worn down, deprived of sleep and peace of mind. Mothers and fathers have found themselves trapped by mounting debts after hardship forced them to borrow money to secure food, medicine, or shelter. Through this campaign, we extend a helping hand to the indebted — paying off the debts that have troubled their lives and freeing them from burdens that have kept them away from their families during this blessed month. Ramadan is an opportunity to mend hearts and restore dignity. Contribute with us so that a father can return to the iftar table with his children.

title: Relieve a Hardship This Ramadan!
overview_text:
Relieve a Hardship This Ramadan!
Severe Burns Changed Rajab’s Life and Features 💔
Rajab, a young man in his early twenties, had recently gotten married to start a family and build a happy life with his wife — a life he had always dreamed of.
But sadly, everything changed in one devastating night. A heater exploded, causing severe burns to his face and foot.
Rajab lost his job and his ability to support his wife. Today, he remains confined to his home, unable to afford the cost of his medical treatment.
Rajab is waiting for your support to help cover the cost of medications needed for skin and burn reconstruction.
Stand with him — and with dozens of others whose bodies were scarred by fire and whose features were forever changed.

title: Coding Education Project in Schools
overview_text:
Coding Education Project in Schools
The world is accelerating, and technology is writing the future in a language only those who learn it early can understand. Our children are growing up in the digital age, but coding is what transforms them from consumers into creators of the future. The opportunity to create this transformation is in our hands today. This project does not target a limited number of students — it lays a sustainable foundation that can be built upon year after year. Developable curricula, reusable learning tools, and a scalable training model that can expand within schools. Over the course of 10 weeks, we introduce coding into classrooms using real devices, guided instruction, and a simplified approach that strengthens logical thinking and creativity. With just $3, you help build a sustainable skill with long-term impact for one child. 👨‍💻 With $30, you help build skills for 10 children. 👩‍💻👨‍💻 With $50, you help 16 children gain a valuable new skill. With $100, you help equip a full classroom (33 students) with future-ready skills. 👩‍🏫📚 Support education… and be part of shaping the future.

title: Ramadan Food Parcels
overview_text: With the arrival of the blessed days of Ramadan, iftar becomes a moment of tranquility and togetherness, as families gather around one table after a long day of fasting. Yet behind this warm image, thousands of families live a harsh reality — waiting anxiously for iftar, unsure if they will find enough to meet their most basic needs. In this month of mercy and solidarity, when rewards are multiplied and الخير rises above all, we launch the 2025 Ramadan Food Parcels Campaign to extend a helping hand to the most vulnerable families, giving them a sense of security and the dignity of a proper iftar. With a donation of just $60, you can provide a complete food parcel containing essential staples — enough to support an entire family throughout the blessed month. Your generosity today is not just assistance; it is hope, a smile, and peace of mind for a family worn down by hardship. Let us fill the nights of Ramadan with الخير and ensure that no home waits for iftar without support.

title: Suhoor for a Family in Ramadan
overview_text:
Suhoor for a Family in Ramadan
During the blessed nights of Ramadan, while many prepare for suhoor to sustain them through the day of fasting, thousands of families welcome this holy month with empty tables and heavy hearts, unable to secure even the most basic food essentials. Harsh living conditions have cast a heavy shadow over vulnerable families, making suhoor a burden beyond their means. For this reason, the Molham Team has launched the Ramadan Suhoor Campaign to provide food baskets containing essential items for suhoor and deliver them to the families most in need — helping them fast with dignity and easing the hardship of their days and nights. With just $20, you can be the reason a struggling family enjoys a dignified suhoor and finds reassurance during this blessed month. Your generosity today creates real impact, turning Ramadan nights into moments of mercy and hope for those awaiting kindness.

title: Community Iftars (Cities & Camps)
overview_text:
Community Iftars (Cities & Camps)
In the blessed month of Ramadan — the month of mercy and family togetherness — iftar is a moment of anticipation and joy, when families gather around food after a long day of fasting. Yet in camps and cities, thousands of families live a different reality, where iftar arrives amid limited resources and the absence of even the most basic stability. For this reason, we are launching the Community Iftar Campaign with families in camps and cities, to share this special Ramadan moment with them and provide a dignified iftar that restores warmth to their days. This initiative is not merely a meal — it is a humanitarian gesture that carries the meaning of mercy and affirms that الخير is still present in this blessed month. With just $6, you can provide iftar for one person, giving them a dignified meal after a long day of fasting. Your contribution can make a real difference and leave a lasting impact on the heart of someone in need this Ramadan.

title: Feed a Fasting
overview_text: In Ramadan, iftar is not only about a prepared table — it is about the reassurance a family feels knowing their day will not pass without a dignified meal. Yet many families enter this blessed month burdened with worry, unable to secure even the most basic cooking ingredients for their iftar. For this reason, the Iftar for a Fasting Family campaign was launched to provide real support to families in need by supplying essential cooking ingredients sufficient for two days, enabling them to prepare their iftar meals with dignity and peace of mind. The basket includes essential vegetables and fresh chicken to meet a family’s daily needs and ease the anxiety that weighs on their hearts before mealtime. With just $20, you can be the reason a struggling family enjoys dignified iftar meals for two days during the blessed month of Ramadan. Your generosity today could mean the difference between worry and relief, between uncertainty and comfort at the moment of iftar.

title: Iftar Campaign
overview_text: When we were children, we used to wait eagerly for the Maghrib call to prayer in Ramadan. Not only for the food our mothers prepared, but for that small box filled with delicious treats, a little fruit, and a cup of juice. It tasted different — and its joy was bigger than its size. With that little box, we truly felt that Ramadan had arrived, and we celebrated with pure childhood happiness. We know very well what that small box means to a child, and how a simple moment can turn iftar into a beautiful memory that stays in the heart. That is why we launched the Children’s Iftar Box Campaign — to prepare simple, child-friendly iftar meals that bring joy after a long day of fasting and allow children to experience the sweetness of Ramadan just like others. A small box… but a big joy ❤️ With just $2, you can draw a genuine smile on a child’s face after fasting and be the reason behind an unforgettable moment of happiness.

title: Food Baskets for Orphan Families
overview_text:
Food Baskets for Orphan Families
A special campaign to provide food baskets for the families of sponsored orphans supported by the Molham Volunteering Team. Another Ramadan approaches children who have been destined to grow up without a father… children who have known little of childhood beyond its name — sitting at tables emptied of dishes, with mothers unable to offer a meal to a hungry child waiting in hope. Because orphanhood was never their choice, and the emptiness of their tables today is our responsibility, we are launching a food basket distribution campaign dedicated to orphan families — providing each family with a basket containing essential supplies to sustain them throughout the blessed month. Contribute with us. Charity never decreases wealth, and no الخير is ever lost when placed in the hands of an orphan.

title: Little Hearts
overview_text: There are children born with congenital heart defects that prevent their hearts from pumping blood normally, causing them to grow up carrying pain in silence. Our little Saad is one of them. His condition worsens with every passing moment, and signs of heart failure are becoming clear: bluish discoloration of the hands and lips, rapid fatigue, shortness of breath, a racing heartbeat, and a childhood taken away because even play exhausts his small heart. Saad, along with many other children, urgently needs life-saving surgeries to restore a regular heartbeat and give them a chance at a normal life. Yet their families stand helpless, unable to bear the high costs of treatment. Through the Little Hearts campaign, we aim to secure the care they desperately need.

title: Zakat Al-Mal in Ramadan
overview_text: Your Zakat… Purification of Your Wealth and Light in Ramadan In the blessed month of Ramadan, the gates of mercy are opened, rewards are multiplied, and deeds are raised to Allah during the greatest days of the year. It is a month of purifying the soul before purifying wealth — a month of giving that never decreases, but instead brings blessings and increase. Zakat Al-Mal is not an optional donation; it is one of the pillars of Islam and a right ordained by Allah in the wealth of the well-off for the poor and those in need. Allah says: “Take from their wealth a charity by which you purify them and cause them increase.” (Qur’an 9:103) In Ramadan, the value of this great pillar is even more significant, as righteous deeds carry greater reward and are nearer to acceptance. Fulfill your Zakat today.

title: Daily Sadaqah - Ramadan 2025
overview_text:
Daily Sadaqah - Ramadan 2025
"Every day in Ramadan, we seek ways to draw closer to Allah, and what could be greater than making charity a daily habit in this blessed month? The Prophet Muhammad said: "The best charity is that given in Ramadan." These sacred days are a golden opportunity to multiply your rewards and make your charity a means to ease someone's hardship, feed the hungry, and bring a smile to a needy face. With just $1 or more per day, you can have an ongoing charity throughout the entire holy month." The amount you wish to donate will be deducted from your account daily throughout the holy month of Ramadan. The deduction will automatically end at the conclusion of Ramadan.

title: Ramadan Waqf for a Mosque
overview_text:
Ramadan Waqf for a Mosque
Many mosques in Syria today suffer from neglect and damage. Some are partially destroyed, while others still stand but lack the most basic necessities such as carpets, lighting, and prayer supplies, due to years without restoration. Allah Almighty says in the Holy Qur’an: “The mosques of Allah are only to be maintained by those who believe in Allah and the Last Day, establish prayer, give zakah, and do not fear except Allah. It is they who are expected to be among the guided.” (Qur’an 9:18) Inspired by this great meaning, we are launching the “Ramadan Waqf for a Mosque” campaign to provide essential supplies for mosques. Throughout the blessed month of Ramadan, we will announce charitable endowment opportunities on our official pages. These can be sponsored by one donor, a family, or a group of friends coming together in الخير. Your donation to this campaign will help provide mosques with essential needs such as carpets, air conditioners, water coolers, lighting, and prayer supplies — making your contribution a continuous charity (Sadaqah Jariyah) in every rak‘ah, every supplication, and every prostration performed in this blessed month. This campaign is an opportunity for your giving to continue even beyond Ramadan.

title: Water for Life in Ramadan
overview_text:
Water for Life in Ramadan
The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “The best charity is giving water to drink.” The Molham Team is launching the “Water for Life in Ramadan” campaign to deliver water to hundreds of fasting individuals before the Maghrib call to prayer, easing their thirst and hardship during the long days of Ramadan. Our campaign includes establishing lasting charitable endowments and water distribution points in mosques, streets, camps, and public spaces — a continuous charity (Sadaqah Jariyah) whose reward continues even after the blessed month of Ramadan ends. With every drop of water you provide to a fasting person, a reward is written for you — a continuous charity that remains in your scale of good deeds, multiplied with every person who drinks from it. Hasten to share in the reward during this blessed month.

title: Feeding a Person in Need This Ramadan!
overview_text:
Feeding a Person in Need This Ramadan!
With every sunset in Ramadan, there is someone waiting for the call to prayer with a heart heavy with need. There are families who welcome Maghrib with anxiety, asking themselves: Will iftar be available today? With your contribution, that anxiety turns into reassurance, and that waiting becomes a sincere prayer for you. Just $10 is enough to provide a dignified meal for a family in need — a meal that carries a sense of security, the warmth of sharing, and the blessings of this holy month. In Ramadan, giving is not measured by its amount, but by its impact. The reward is multiplied, and the الخير reaches farther than we can imagine. Be the reason for a smile at the call to Maghrib. Be a partner in relieving someone’s hunger. Do not let this month pass without leaving a real impact on the lives of those who are waiting for kindness. Contribute to the Feeding a Person in Need campaign, and make their iftar more dignified through your generosity.

title: Sponsor a Student This Ramadan!
overview_text:
Sponsor a Student This Ramadan!
Another night among the greatest of nights each of them could be the night. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “Seek Laylat al-Qadr in the odd nights of the last ten nights of Ramadan.” During these nights, when mercy descends and rewards are multiplied, even a small act can become a source of immense reward that lasts for years. Amani is a young girl who lost her father and lives in the Molham Village. She endured long periods of displacement and instability. Despite everything she has gone through, she still holds firmly to her dream of learning and continues her education today at Molham School. Amani’s story is just one among many stories of children who insist on continuing their education despite the hardships. Supporting their education is not just temporary assistance—it is a real opportunity for a better future. Perhaps your contribution during these blessed nights will coincide with Laylat al-Qadr, and Allah will write for you a great reward that continues as long as beneficial knowledge remains. “Wealth does not decrease because of charity.” So make sure you have a share of goodness during these most hopeful nights. ✨

title: Support for an Orphaned Student!
overview_text:
Support for an Orphaned Student!
When a father is lost, the road becomes heavier and dreams feel more distant. Yet education alone can restore to an orphan what was taken from them. Supporting them today means saving an entire future. Despite their young age, harsh circumstances, and the weight they carry, they hold tightly to their education with determination. Today, hundreds of children stand at a crossroads — either they continue their education with a helping hand extended to them, or they are forced to drop out and bury their dreams before they have the chance to grow. This is where our role begins. In Ramadan 2026, we launch the “Support an Orphaned Student” campaign to be the support they need and the hand that paves their path toward a brighter future. Sponsoring an orphan is a great act of worship — and in Ramadan, its reward is multiplied, its impact is greater, and it becomes a lasting charity. Contribute today.

title: Last ten nights campaign
overview_text: As we approach the last ten nights of the blessed month of Ramadan, we launch the "Last Ten: Zakat & Sadaqah" campaign to dedicate your Zakat, Fitr, and charity during these sacred days. Through this campaign, we aim to share your generosity with families in Syria and Gaza. By clicking the Donate button, you can choose the type of contribution you wish to make: Zakat al-Mal Zakat al-Fitr Sadaqah (Charity) Every donation you make will be a lifeline for those in need during these blessed days. Donate now and be their source of goodness this Ramadan.

title: Ramadan in Gaza
overview_text: Let your generosity in Ramadan reach our people in Gaza. Thousands of families, exhausted by the hardships of war and dire living conditions, are struggling during the holy month. Molham Volunteering Team in Gaza is working around the clock to ease the suffering of displaced families within the strip. They are providing essential needs through the charity kitchen, distributing food supplies (including vegetable baskets and canned goods), delivering water, and assisting urgent humanitarian cases. You can also donate and contribute to alleviating their hardship. Additionally, we are striving to cover the needs of some medical cases evacuated for treatment outside Gaza, whether through medical expenses or living costs. Remember, even your smallest contribution makes a difference.

title: Molham Center for Speech and Rehabilitation of People with Disabilities
overview_text:
Molham Center for Speech and Rehabilitation of People with Disabilities
Three children—Mudar, Mohammad, and Ghazal—are living with muscle atrophy, brain injuries, and motor developmental delay. They belong to a small family whose sole provider struggles every day to meet even their most basic needs—diapers, medication, and food—amid extremely harsh economic and living conditions. The father’s work brings in only a meager income, barely enough to keep hunger at bay for his children. Meanwhile, the family is unable to provide the consistent medical care that these children so urgently require. These three children are suffering in silence. Stand with them, and help ease their pain and support their family, which feels helpless in the face of their children’s suffering. A contribution of just $400 could make a meaningful difference—helping cover essential needs and easing the burden on their father. Be there for them and for dozens of other children whose lives have been limited by disability.

title: Whoever saves a life
overview_text: Let Your Help for Khadija Be a Good Deed to End Your Ramadan Khadija is an infant girl who was born with hydrocephalus, a condition that caused her to be hospitalized and undergo surgery to implant a brain shunt device. All of this pain for our little girl, while her family continues to struggle and do their best to keep up with her treatment despite their limited financial means. Due to the infections Khadija suffered, the device had to be removed. Today, she urgently needs a new shunt so her condition does not worsen and lead to permanent disability. Your contribution can help provide this life-saving shunt device. This amount could mean an entire lifetime for a little girl who has not yet reached her first birthday. Many of our children have their childhoods burdened by illness. Your support in helping them recover gives them the chance for a better life—one they truly deserve.

title: From the Michigan Community to the Children of Syria
overview_text:
From the Michigan Community to the Children of Syria
For the child who left a piece of their childhood behind in a schoolyard struck by bombing… for playgrounds that once echoed with laughter… And for the teacher whose students were forced to leave, taking the last lessons with them… For them, we write a “New Line” — a journey to restore schools and bring children back to their playgrounds and classrooms. Together, at this charity iftar, we will contribute to rebuilding Syria’s schools and become a means of returning thousands of students to their desks. Through education, nations are built and rise again. With it, we draw a brighter future for a country that has endured pain and hardship.

title: Sadaqah Jariayh (Qusai Hamid)
overview_text: The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) said: "When a person dies, all their deeds end except three: a continuing charity (Sadaqah Jariyah), beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who prays for them." With hearts full of faith in God’s will and souls overflowing with loyalty to those who have left us but remain in our hearts, the Community in Columbus announces the launch of a campaign to renovate a school in Syria. This project aims to be a beacon of knowledge, a foundation for hope, and a continuous charity (Sadaqah Jariyah) whose rewards reach our late brother, Qusai Hamed. Qusai was a man who loved goodness, and we believe that education is a light that never fades. In our homeland, thousands of children are waiting for a safe place to learn. By renovating this school, we are not just rebuilding walls; we are building the future of a generation and planting a tree of rewards that is watered every morning by the prayers of students. May its peace and light reach the soul of our beloved Qusai.

title: 1000 Supporting 1000 Campaign
overview_text: In this blessed month, while the days are adorned with the spirit of Ramadan and families gather around their tables, there are still homes burdened by silence, missing the comfort of reassurance. They are not in need of decorative lights, but of renewed hope and relief that enters the hearts of their loved ones after long hardship. There is a mother calculating what little food remains, a father worried about the cost of medicine, and children waiting for a month in which they can feel joy like others. Each home’s need may differ, but the pain is the same — and so is the hope. Your donation of $200 is not a temporary gesture; it opens the door to peace within an entire household, securing food, medicine, and essential needs for a family throughout the month. It is a gift that restores smiles and reminds them they are not alone. Some homes need food to sustain them, others need treatment to ease their pain — and your support will reach those most in need with care and integrity, making every act of giving from you the beginning of new hope.

title: The Gratitude Campaign for the Faculty of Pharmacy – University of Aleppo
overview_text:
The Gratitude Campaign for the Faculty of Pharmacy – University of Aleppo
The Gratitude Campaign for the Faculty of Pharmacy – University of Aleppo Peace, mercy, and blessings of Allah be upon you. To my fellow Syrian pharmacists in Germany, The Faculty of Pharmacy at the University of Aleppo is not just an ordinary name in the history of Syrian pharmacy. Over many decades, it has graduated distinguished scientific and professional figures who have left their mark in Syria and beyond. However, during the years of the revolution and what followed, under a system that did not invest in education or in people, the Assad regime, neglect accumulated, resources declined, and the faculty began operating with tools far below the minimum required for modern pharmaceutical education. Today, there is a serious attempt to rebuild what has been damaged. The new administration and the faculty dean aim to make the Faculty of Pharmacy in Aleppo capable of keeping pace with international universities. Of course, this requires equipment, supplies, and resources that allow lectures to be delivered properly, training to truly be training, and laboratories to perform their function rather than remain merely a name on the door. The reality is also that the state treasury is weak and official support is limited. Therefore, the faculty relies—whenever possible—on its graduates. Many of us here in Germany are graduates of this faculty or know its value and understand what it means for an educational institution of this scale to be left without the necessary tools. For this reason, we are launching a " donation campaign to support the Faculty of Pharmacy – University of Aleppo", to provide essential supplies that will help the faculty restore its educational capacity. This campaign is being carried out in coordination with the Dean of the Faculty of Pharmacy at the University of Aleppo, Dr. Mohammad Yasser Abji. The items currently needed include: - Desktop computers: 10 units - approximately $3,000 - 55-inch TV screens: 3 units - approximately $1,200 - Camera surveillance system for the faculty - approximately $4,000 - Metal doors to protect exam and research sections - approximately $1,000 - Solar power energy system - approximately $7,000 - Internal internet network connections - approximately $1,500 - Additional water tanks for laboratories - approximately $1,000 - First-aid kits for laboratories: 15 units - $300 Supporting the faculty of pharmacy is not simply a donation to a building; it is support for a long chain: students who learn, graduate, and then provide healthcare services to people for years to come. Beneficial knowledge is among the greatest forms of ongoing charity. What you give today may tomorrow become knowledge that saves a patient or leads to the correct medical decision. Its reward and blessings will continue to reach you until the Day of Judgment. May Allah reward you with goodness and elevate the status of knowledge and its people through you.

Beneficial knowledge is among the greatest forms of ongoing charity. What you give today may tomorrow become knowledge that saves a patient or leads to the correct medical decision. Its reward and blessings will continue to reach you until the Day of Judgment. May Allah reward you with goodness and elevate the status of knowledge and its people through you.

title: Sponsor an orphan in Syria !
overview_text:
Sponsor an orphan in Syria !
“I have 10 orphans on my shoulders… I want to raise them and not deprive them of anything, but by God, life is hard, and my age no longer allows me to work and provide everything for them. My heart aches for them—they have never known a father’s tenderness nor true support.”
With these words, Aunt Shamous summed up the reality she lives in with her orphaned grandchildren and the burdens weighing on her—along with her illnesses and the difficulty of securing her grandchildren’s needs, which only deepens her pain and sense of helplessness.
But with your monthly sponsorship of
for her grandchildren, you can help ease her burden by providing their most basic needs and making them feel the true support they so desperately need.
Your donation—of any amount—can greatly help many orphans and provide for their needs.

But with your monthly sponsorship of $50 for her grandchildren, you can help ease her burden by providing their most basic needs and making them feel the true support they so desperately need.

Your donation—of any amount—can greatly help many orphans and provide for their needs.

title: Zakat Alfitr
overview_text: Children dreaming of a simple joy, and mothers worrying about how to welcome Eid while their tables remain empty. Zakat Al-Fitr is not merely a charity — it is a message of mercy and a caring hand that arrives at the right time. Through our campaign, we ensure that Zakat Al-Fitr contributions from generous donors are delivered directly to the families most in need before Eid Al-Fitr, becoming a reason for happiness to enter their hearts.

title: Water for Gaza
overview_text: In Gaza… Where thirst is not a metaphor, but a daily reality. Where water is scarce, saline, or completely unavailable… And where a fasting person, just before the Maghrib call to prayer, is not searching for a table full of food, but for a safe cup of water to quench their thirst after a long day under siege and deprivation. Amid the harsh humanitarian conditions faced by our people in the Gaza Strip — with damaged infrastructure and disrupted access to clean water — obtaining safe water has become a daily struggle, especially during the month of Ramadan. The Molham Team is launching the “Quench Their Thirst” campaign to deliver water to fasting individuals in Gaza before the Maghrib call to prayer, easing their suffering from thirst during the long days of Ramadan.

title: Zakat Al-Mal for Our People in Gaza!
overview_text:
Zakat Al-Mal for Our People in Gaza!
In the blessed month of Ramadan, the gates of mercy are opened, rewards are multiplied, and deeds are raised to Allah during the greatest days of the year. It is a month of devotion and purification — a month of fulfilling rights and giving in ways that Allah blesses, turning little into abundance. Zakat Al-Mal is a great pillar of Islam and a prescribed right for the poor and those in need. Allah says: “Establish prayer and give zakah, and whatever good you put forward for yourselves—you will find it with Allah.” (Surah Al-Baqarah: 110) Amid the harsh humanitarian conditions faced by our people in the Gaza Strip, Zakat today becomes security for families who have lost their sources of income, relief for children waiting for their daily sustenance, and support for homes worn down by poverty and hardship. Pay your Zakat now — make it a purification of your wealth, a relief for them, and a blessing whose impact extends in this life and the Hereafter.

title: Share Your Meal
overview_text: In the holy month of Ramadan, the month of generosity and giving, let us share our tables with families in the camps. During the month of Ramadan, thousands of Syrians in the camps face difficulty in securing their breakfast. With rising prices and scarcity of resources, securing the needs for Ramadan has become a heavy burden on many families. But we can make a difference! This campaign is to share breakfast tables with our people in the camps. You can share the meal you want with a family in need.

title: Be a Support
overview_text: The revolution has prevailed — but have we stood by those who made it possible? They preserved our dignity — have we preserved theirs? They restored our homeland — have we restored their homes? Not everyone who sacrificed for the revolution returned to their house. Not everyone who was freed from ظلم found safety. There are forgotten families living in worn-out tents, between raging winds and flooding rains. They are the trust of the revolution’s heroes. They are the trust of one million martyrs. They are the trust of two million missing. Let us be among those who contribute. Let us support our steadfast brothers and sisters and return them safely to their homes. Abu Huraira (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “Whoever conceals (the faults of) a Muslim, Allah will conceal him in this world and the Hereafter. Whoever relieves a believer’s hardship, Allah will relieve his hardship on the Day of Resurrection. And Allah aids the servant as long as the servant aids his brother.” (Narrated by Muslim) We — Sanabel Al-Khair 🌾 — in cooperation with the Molham Volunteering Team, announce the launch of our ninth project: “Be a Support” A project aimed at repairing damaged homes and restoring them to livable conditions. The cost of repairing one home is $4,000 for an entire family, covering: • Roof and wall repairs • Water and sewage network repairs • Window and glass repairs • Transportation costs from tent to home 🎯 $12,000 🏡 3 homes 📍 Kherbet Al-Joz (Idlib/Lattakia countryside) Your donation today… means the end of suffering and the warmth of a home.

title: New Chapter !
overview_text: For the child who left a piece of their childhood in a schoolyard that was bombed… For playgrounds that once echoed with laughter… For a teacher whose students were forced to leave, taking the last lessons with them… For them, we begin today a “New Line” — a journey to restore schools and bring children back to their playgrounds and classrooms. Through education, our nations are built and rise again. With it, we draw a brighter future for a land that has endured pain and hardship. You can donate: $500 – Provide classroom desks for one classroom, ensuring a comfortable learning environment. $1,000 – Renovate and repaint one classroom to create an inspiring space for students. $2,000 – Fully renovate and equip one classroom. $4,000 – Fully renovate and equip two classrooms with essential educational materials. $10,000 – Renovate five classrooms, improving the learning experience for hundreds of children. $25,000 – Renovate half a school, helping hundreds of students return to education. $50,000 – Fully renovate an entire school, transforming the lives of a whole generation and securing a better future for them. Donate now and help a student return to school.

title: Gaza School !
overview_text: Molham Village in the countryside of A’zaz has welcomed hundreds of families, including orphaned children who all share one dream: to continue their education. To make this dream a reality, Molham Village School was established and today embraces hundreds of students. Among these children are those who lost their families to war, and others who lost their fathers in brutal airstrikes while they were only months old. These children, with their big dreams, need your support to help them achieve them. With a contribution of just $25, you can sponsor a student for one month and help them build their future.

title: For the very first time, Al-Jahbeth arrives in Gothenburg.
overview_text:
For the very first time, Al-Jahbeth arrives in Gothenburg.
The Molham Volunteering Team in Sweden invites you to a stand-up show "Mo komedi" with Bisher Najjar, “Al-Jahbeth,” a night blending laughter, reflection, and dialogue… From Syria, and back to Syria. The evening will also feature the short film “Hariboon Bil-Fitra” directed by Mohammed Talawi, followed by an open discussion with the audience. 📆 Date: April 5, 2026 🕑 Time: 17:30 📍 Location: Kungsportsavenyen 45, Göteborg All proceeds from the evening will support refugees and reconstruction projects in Syria through the Molham Team.

title: Friends of Cancer Patients Campaign – Daraa
overview_text:
Friends of Cancer Patients Campaign – Daraa
The ‘Friends of Cancer Patients’ campaign is a community-driven initiative launched by activists in Daraa to support cancer patients who lack access to comprehensive treatment within the governorate. The campaign aims to ease the financial burden on patients by covering part of the costs of transportation, medical tests, and essential medications, while also advocating for sustainable solutions in the healthcare sector in southern Syria. Donations are collected through multiple channels, including a unified network of field collection boxes, direct financial transfers, in-kind contributions, and digital fundraising campaigns. Guided by full transparency and accountability, the initiative emphasizes that this is a community effort to relieve suffering, not a definitive solution.

title: Sharing Iftar with Our Orphaned Children.
overview_text:
Sharing Iftar with Our Orphaned Children.
With the arrival of the blessed month of Ramadan—a season of compassion and generosity—the Molham Team warmly invites you to take part in our newest humanitarian initiative: “Iftar for an Orphaned Child.” Through this initiative, we hope to host a communal iftar gathering for orphaned children, creating a moment of joy and togetherness that reminds them they are not alone. In this sacred month, we aim to fill their evening with warmth, laughter, and a sense of belonging within a caring community. We invite you to support this heartfelt effort by contributing: $5 to provide iftar for one orphaned child, or $7 as a generous contribution toward making this gathering possible.

details_text:
“Build it Campaign” is a fundraising campaign to rehabilitate Kafr Naboudeh Third School in the town of Kafr Naboudeh, northern rural Hama, after the extensive destruction the city and its schools suffered during the years of war.

With thousands of families now returning to Kafr Naboudeh, there is an urgent need for a safe and properly equipped school to accommodate children who are currently receiving their education in temporary spaces lacking the most basic facilities.

The campaign aims to fully restore and rehabilitate the school and equip it to provide a safe and stable learning environment for more than 500 students, supporting the stability of the returning community and protecting children from the risks of educational deprivation.

title: Dress Orphaned Children in the Joy of Eid.
overview_text:
Dress Orphaned Children in the Joy of Eid.
Our children are the joy that gives our celebrations their meaning. But can Eid truly feel like Eid for them without the gift of new clothes? The answer is no. From our special Eidiya campaign to this heartfelt clothing drive, every detail of our children’s happiness is carefully woven together. For far too many years, the children of our homeland have lived through long seasons of Eid beneath the shadow of bombardment, within the walls of fear and displacement—deprived of the simplest rights of childhood. Many have grown up without truly knowing what Eid feels like, watching celebrations pass by like distant memories they never had the chance to live. That is why it remains our duty—and our promise—to give them the Eids they deserve: joyful days filled with laughter, wonder, and beautiful new clothes after years of being denied them. Let them shine like little princesses and brave young heroes, stepping into Eid with radiant smiles and bright new outfits.

title: Students for Students
overview_text: Students for Students is a fundraising campaign led by the Syrian Students’ Union in Canada (SSUC) in partnership with Molham Team and Syrian student groups across Canada. The campaign unites Syrian students in Canada to support their peers in Syria, helping them continue their education despite difficult circumstances.

Together, through this charity Iftar and in partnership with SAC, we will contribute to rebuilding schools in Syria, helping thousands of students return to their seats of learning and contributing to the rebuilding of Syria, so it can once again become a safe, thriving country filled with hope and peace. title: Ramadan Food Parcels
overview_text: This Ramadan Ibn Sina Society and the Syrian Association at RSU, in partnership with Molham Team, are honored to launch a joint charity campaign dedicated to supporting vital humanitarian projects in Syria. As we enter this blessed month of mercy and generosity, we are reminded that every act of charity is a seed planted for the Hereafter. Let us be the reason someone smiles. Let us be the mercy that reaches a broken heart. Let us be the hands that carry hope to Syria. Allah ﷻ says:“The example of those who spend their wealth seeking the pleasure of Allah and assuring [reward for] themselves is like a garden on high ground…”— Qur’an 2:265 And the Prophet ﷺ said:“Charity does not decrease wealth.”— (Muslim) For many struggling families in Syria — including displaced families living in tents — Ramadan is not only a month of fasting, but a month of hardship. Through this initiative, we will deliver essential food parcels containing staple items that allow families to:•⁠ ⁠Break their fast with dignity•⁠ ⁠Prepare suḥūr and ifṭār without worry•⁠ ⁠Experience the joy and peace of Ramadan The Prophet ﷺ said “Whoever gives ifṭār to a fasting person will have a reward like his, without decreasing anything from the reward of the fasting person.”— (Tirmidhi) Imagine earning the reward of every fast broken through your generosity.

title: Orphanage Support
overview_text: This Ramadan Ibn Sina Society and the Syrian Association at RSU, in partnership with Molham Team, are honored to launch a joint charity campaign dedicated to supporting vital humanitarian projects in Syria. “The example of those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah is like a seed of grain that grows seven ears; in every ear is a hundred grains. And Allah multiplies for whom He wills.” — Qur’an 2:261 Thousands of children in Syria have lost their fathers due to years of conflict. Behind every statistic is a child in need of stability, care, education, and dignity. Through this project, we aim to stand beside orphaned children by supporting orphanage initiatives that provide: •⁠ ⁠Safe and nurturing environments •⁠ ⁠Educational support •⁠ ⁠Daily care and essential needs •⁠ ⁠Emotional and spiritual development The Prophet ﷺ emphasized the immense virtue of caring for orphans: “I and the one who cares for an orphan will be like this in Paradise,” and he held his two fingers together. — (Bukhari) Your contribution is not simply a donation — it is an investment in a child’s future, a means of restoring hope, and a path to closeness with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in Jannah. 🌿 Be a Source of Mercy This Ramadan Ramadan is the month in which rewards are multiplied, sins are forgiven, and hearts are softened. The Prophet ﷺ was described as being even more generous in Ramadan than at any other time. This is your opportunity to: •⁠ ⁠Multiply your rewards •⁠ ⁠Relieve hardship from vulnerable families •⁠ ⁠Support orphaned children in rebuilding their lives •⁠ ⁠Leave a legacy of ongoing charity (ṣadaqah jāriyah) Every donation — large or small — carries immense weight with Allah ﷻ.

title: Helping someone in distress
overview_text: Her Little Face Was Burned… and Her Features Are Gone A heavy trial and painful helplessness is what brother Ahmed is living through after fire burned his little daughter’s face, disfiguring it and taking away her features. She now suffers from severe physical and psychological pain, and Ahmed stands helpless, unable to ease her suffering. Ahmed is one of the heroes of our country who stays awake day and night protecting our homeland and safeguarding its security. Despite his long and exhausting work and his efforts to provide for his children, the difficult economic situation stands in his way. Then this painful ordeal struck his beloved daughter, making the hardship even heavier and more heartbreaking. Today, our little girl urgently needs medications and various medical treatment expenses that Ahmed cannot afford. Your donation can help provide these necessities and relieve the distress of this father and his daughter. Please stand with them and with us to help many families who are living under difficult circumstances and cannot afford the medical needs of their sick children.

title: A Day Filled with Mercy!
overview_text:
A Day Filled with Mercy!
In the displacement tents, dozens of patients endure harsh and exhausting health conditions, where illnesses worsen due to limited resources and difficult access to medical care. Many cases require urgent intervention—from surgeries that cannot be postponed to medications and tests that could save lives. Yet financial barriers stand in the way of treatment, leaving families unable to cover medical costs while the danger grows day by day for their most precious children. This is the reality for this young girl and her brother, both suffering from thalassemia, in need of medications, blood transfusions, and costly care that their father cannot afford. This campaign is for them and for every patient facing similar struggles. Remember: your contribution today could save a life—don’t wait.

title: Eidiya & Eid Outfit
overview_text: Eid is nothing but the joy of a displaced child in their new clothes, eagerly awaiting their Eid gift… so that the holiday doesn’t pass like any other day. The “Eidiya” campaign comes to make a real difference for children, bringing them happiness directly from your hearts to theirs! We will provide Eid clothes and gifts for hundreds of children in northern Syria, refugee-hosting countries, and Gaza, to paint smiles on faces that war has stolen. With a $40 donation, you can provide Eid clothes for one child, or give an Eidiya gift for just $3. Countless smiles and moments of joy are waiting for you to create them.

title: “Eid Sweets"
overview_text: As Eid approaches, thousands of children in the camps are waiting for a small joy that reminds them that Eid still brings hope. Today, we launch the “Eid Sweets” campaign to bring smiles to the faces of families in the camps by providing Eid treats for the children. 💛 Cost of one treat package: only $10 Each package brings a moment of joy to a child and a warm Eid for a family facing hardship. With your small contribution, we can make a big difference. Join us in spreading the joy of Eid and reminding them: you are not alone.

title: Eid toys.
overview_text: This campaign is dedicated to bringing smiles to children’s faces this Eid. Donate a gift and make a child’s Eid unforgettable!

title: From Syrian community in South of Florida
overview_text:
From Syrian community in South of Florida
With the generous invitation of the respected Al-Ayoubi family in Florida, and in collaboration with SAAPP Organization, Molham Team is honored to be present today to share this special Ramadan evening and join the Al-Ayoubi family and their distinguished guests for a charitable Iftar dedicated to a meaningful cause. This gathering aims to support the project of rehabilitating schools in Syria, as thousands of children still dream of returning to their classrooms, carrying their books again, and starting their school days in a safe environment that gives them the chance to learn and build their future. Your contribution today is more than just a donation—it is a powerful act of hope that helps reopen the doors of education for thousands of students waiting for the opportunity to continue their studies and pursue their dreams. Together, we can restore their right to education… and together, we can build a brighter future for an entire generation

title: Together for Syria’s Future
overview_text: Together, Molham Volunteering Team and SAC invite you to be part of a meaningful campaign to help rebuild Syria. Join us in turning compassion into action, and in building a future where every child has a place to learn and give our people in Syria hope and rebuilding lives. Your support will directly contribute to the rebuilding of schools in Syria, creating safe spaces where children can learn, grow, and dream again. At the same time, it helps amplify the collective efforts of communities here in USA working toward a stronger, more hopeful future for Syria. Every contribution is more than a donation — it is a step toward education, stability, prosperity and long-term impact. Join us in turning compassion into action, and in building a future where every child has a place to learn and give our people in syira a chance to thrive.

title: From Oslo To Syria !
overview_text:
From Oslo To Syria !
In a faraway place, along a long road that passes through many cities and countries, a bicycle will move — and with it, hope for hundreds of students in Syria. Alaa, a young Syrian man, decided to take on a different journey from Oslo to Damascus on his bicycle. The trip will last three months. He carries a simple message: education is worth traveling long distances for. Alaa was once a child who had to leave his country. He was given the chance to study in his new home, and it changed his life. Today, he is returning to give the same opportunity to others. In Syria, more than 2.4 million children are out of school. Millions more study in difficult conditions or without a proper learning environment. Donate and help us repair Al-Abbadah School in the Damascus countryside, so we can bring life back to it for hundreds of students.

title: Urgent Medical Case !
overview_text: “Every day this cyst keeps growing, becoming bigger than her whole body… my daughter is dying before my eyes, and I can’t ease her pain or end her suffering…” These words from Aisha’s mother capture all the anguish and heartbreak she carries, and what her little girl is going through with meningocele - a condition that is stealing her childhood and keeping her from her mother’s embrace. Aisha urgently needs a brain shunt device to improve her condition, return to her mother’s arms, and live her childhood free from pain and suffering. Your donation today, no matter how small, will help ease her pain, support her family, and contribute to helping similar medical cases

title: Urgent Humanitarian Cases
overview_text: A little heart in pain and a family waiting for someone to bring them relief. Just fourteen days after Ahmad’s birth, it was discovered that he was battling a blood infection. He now lies in an incubator, surrounded by his father’s fear and helplessness, and his mother’s hope and unceasing prayers. Ahmad’s father is staying by his child’s side, awaiting his recovery, which has affected his work, plunged him into debt, and made life increasingly difficult for him. This is not just one family’s story, but the story of many families whose souls are waiting for someone to ease their pain. Your donation today can make the difference between suffering and survival. Be the reason for their recovery and bring comfort to their hearts.

title: From the Ruins of Destruction to Restored Homes: The Kabbineh Response
overview_text:
From the Ruins of Destruction to Restored Homes: The Kabbineh Response
Once a steadfast shield for the revolutionaries, Kabbineh is now a village reduced to desolation. It endured what its enemies could not overcome, and for that, it was punished by bombardment after bombardment, aircraft following aircraft until not a single stone was left standing and nothing remained but ruin and devastation.Today, the village shows no signs of life, yet its people still carry the hope of returning home. Years of displacement have taken their toll, leaving families unable to repair their homes or rebuild what little remains of their village. This is where our shared responsibility begins: to stand with the people of Kabbineh and repay the village for all it has endured by restoring roads, homes, and mosques, rebuilding essential infrastructure, and helping displaced families return, so Kabbineh can once again come alive with its people. Your donation can replace a tent with a home. Take action today to rebuild Kabbineh and bring an end to the suffering of its displaced families.

title: Zakat al-Mal
overview_text: Allah Almighty says: “And establish prayer and give zakah, and whatever good you put forward for yourselves, you will find it with Allah. Indeed, Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.” [2]Allah, may He be praised and exalted, has ordained zakah to be a means by which one brother supports another relieving his distress and easing his hardship. Today, as the need in Syria continues to grow, and as families return to their destroyed villages after long years of displacement and devastation, we launch the “Zakat al-Mal” campaign. Through this campaign, we receive your zakah contributions, which are distributed to those entitled to them among the needy, the displaced, the sick, orphans, students of knowledge, and homeless families in accordance with the prescribed categories of zakah, throughout the year.
